title: 2022 Hull FC season
wikitext:
{{Short description|English rugby league season}} {{Use dmy dates|date=November 2024}} {{Use British English|date=November 2024}} {{Infobox rugby league team season | name = 2022 Hull F.C. season | year = 2022 | team_colour = black; border: solid white 2px | font_colour = white | league = Super League | league_link = [[Super League XXVII]] | league_rank = 9th | league_wins = | league_draws = | league_losses = | points_for = | points_against = | playoff_result = | cup = Challenge Cup | cup_result = Quarter-finals | cup_wins = | cup_losses = | CEO_title = Chairman | CEO = [[Adam Pearson (sports executive)|Adam Pearson]] | coach_title = Head Coach | coach = [[Brett Hodgson]] | captain = [[Danny Houghton]] | stadium = [[KCOM Stadium]]
[[Kingston upon Hull|Hull]], [[East Riding of Yorkshire]] | agg_attendance = | avg_attendance = | high_attendance = | low_attendance = | top_tries = | top_goals = | top_points = | prev_season = [[2021 Hull FC season|2021]] | next_season = [[2023 Hull FC season|2023]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Hull F.C. seasons |pattern_la1= |pattern_b1= |pattern_ra1= |pattern_sh1 = |pattern_so1 = |leftarm1= |body1 = |rightarm1 = |shorts1 = |socks1 = |pattern_la2 = |pattern_b2= |pattern_ra2= |pattern_sh2 = |pattern_so2 = |leftarm2= |body2= |rightarm2= |shorts2= |socks2= }} In the 2022 rugby league season, [[Hull F.C.]] competed in [[Super League XXVII]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]]. They were coached by [[Brett Hodgson]]. ==Season review== On 13 February, Hull began their Super League campaign with a 16–12 away win at [[Wakefield Trinity]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36608/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-1 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 1 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} before suffering a 38–6 loss to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36653/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-2 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 2 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} An inconsistent first half of the season ensued with a win over [[Salford Red Devils]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36688/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League round-up|publisher=RFL |date=28 February 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} and loss at [[Castleford Tigers]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36745/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=6 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} followed by a run of three wins which included a [[Challenge Cup]] victory over [[Sheffield Eagles]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36784/betfred-super-league-round-five-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round Five Round-Up|publisher=RFL |date=13 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36835/betfred-super-league-round-six-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round Six Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=20 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36891/betfred-challenge-cup-round-6-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Round 6 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=27 March 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} A run of three losses followed, beginning with a golden point 19–18 loss to [[Wigan Warriors]] in the league,{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36949/betfred-super-league-round-7-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 7 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=3 April 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} a quarter-final cup defeat to [[Huddersfield Giants]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37006/betfred-challenge-cup-quarter-final-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup quarter-final round-up |publisher=RFL |date=10 April 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} and a visit to local rivals [[Hull Kingston Rovers]],{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37060/report-hull-kr-16-4-hull-fc |title=Report: Hull KR 16-4 Hull FC |publisher=RFL |date=15 April 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} but this was countered by three more wins in late April.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37169/betfred-super-league-round-11-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 11 round-up |publisher=RFL |date=1 May 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} Hull had a poor second half of the season, failing to win during June and with only two wins in July; a 34–28 win against Hull KR at the [[Magic Weekend]],{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60469/betfred-super-league-round-18-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 18 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=11 July 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} and 30–6 win over [[Toulouse Olympique]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60644/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-21|title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 21 |publisher=RFL |date=1 August 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} In August, Hull had a run of four defeats before again facing Toulouse to record a 28–12 win{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60825/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}}publisher=RFL |date=30 August 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} and then ended their season with a 36–4 loss to Hull KR.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60859/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=3 September 2022 |access-date=15 February 2024}} ==Results== ===Pre-season friendlies=== {{legend2|#CFC|Win|border=1px solid #000000|text=W}} {{legend2|#FFE3E3|Loss|border=1px
